title: Dissection and Immunohistochemistry of Mouse Vagal Ganglia
author: Thomas Taylor-Clark
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.baumieu6

steps.instruction: 6 to 8 weeks old mice (male) were euthanized by CO 2 inhalation.
Mice were transcardially perfused with phosphate buffered saline (PBS) to remove blood followed by 3.7% formaldehyde (PFA) in PBS.
Vagal ganglia (including both jugular and nodose ganglia) were collected and fixed with 3.7% PFA for 2 hours on ice.
Vagal ganglia were transferred to 18% sucrose solution for overnight at 4 °C.
Tissue were mounted in optimal cutting temperature compound and frozen
Tissue were cut at 20 µm thickness and collected onto superfrost plus microscopy slide glass
Tissue were washed with PBS three times for 10 min.
Tissue were blocked with blocking buffer (1% bovine serum albumin/10% normal donkey serum/0.3% triton x-100 in PBS) for 45 min at room temperature.

Tissue were incubated primary antibodies diluted in blocking buffer overnight at 4 °C. Set 1. Rabbit anti-TrkA (1:300, 06-574, Millipore) and Goat anti-VR1 (1:150, SC-12498, Santa Cruz) Set 2. Rabbit anti-TrkA (1:300, 06-574, Millipore) and Goat anti-TrkB (1:300, AF1494, R&D Systems)
Tissue were washed with 0.2% tween20 in PBS (PBST) for 10 min for three times at room temperature.

Secondary antibodies were diluted in the 1% bovine serum albumin/5% normal donkey serum/0.2% PBST and tissue were incubated with it for 1 hour at room temperature. 1. Alexa Fluor 488 donkey anti-rabbit (1:300, A21206, Invitrogen) 2. Alexa Fluor 647 Chicken anti-Goat (1:300, A21469, Invitrogen)
Tissue were washed with 0.2% tween20 in PBS (PBST) for 10 min for three times at room temperature.
Tissue were cover-slipped with DPX for imaging and left to dry in the dark overnight.
